validation.summary: No whitespace errors in T005/T006 contract and implementation paths.

review.evidence: The Kernel, project-scope, dynamic-risk, ownership, compatibility, and canonical lock boundaries were reviewed together. Existing registry, run, runtime-state, process, port, and ownership stores remain authoritative; adapters receive no authorization role; all migrated sync/async owners use one ordered lock implementation; targeted and full regressions pass.

obligation_evidence.evidence: createApplicationKernel is the planned semantic mutation entry and delegates effects to existing handlers/stores; no second lifecycle or state backend was introduced.
Existing CLI/control-plane runtime owners were migrated in place to the shared lock authority, and all legacy lifecycle regressions pass.
evaluateOperationPolicy permits task mutation only when effective runtime risk is low, ownership is verified, scope is resolved, and compatibility permits writes.
Kernel mutation handlers execute only after validation, initial policy evaluation, canonical lock acquisition, and fresh under-lock revalidation.
Adapter annotations are not policy inputs and cannot override Kernel risk, ownership, scope, or compatibility decisions.
Unknown, external, probable, stale, or otherwise unverified ownership cannot reach mutation effects.
Component digest or write-axis mismatch blocks writes while bounded capability and diagnosis queries remain available.
Effective task risk is re-read from trusted project state before effects; medium, high, and destructive risks are refused.
Project context distinguishes resolved, missing, ambiguous, conflicting, out-of-scope, and unconfigured cases and ignores Plugin cwd.
Compatibility checks component and registry/schema digests independently from package/runtime version labels.
Direct sync lifecycle and asynchronous global control-plane mutations now share one canonical risk/lock boundary instead of separate lock-order implementations.
Host approval and descriptive annotations never elevate the under-lock policy result.
Executable project scope comes only from explicit/trusted registered context and cannot cross the allowed workspace roots or inherit Plugin installation cwd.
